description: Confounder: The model assumes .loc in-place modification propagates back to the original Dat

_meta.explanation: The rule 'Using .loc with a boolean mask and column name in a single indexing operation (df.loc[mask, 'col'] = value) reliably modifies the DataFrame in-place, unlike chained assignment.' does NOT hold here. The model assumes .loc in-place modification propagates back to the original DataFrame, but df2 is already a copy created by the boolean slice, so modifying df2 with .loc never affects df.
_meta.curriculum_note: Rule 'Using .loc with a boolean mask and column name in a single indexing operation (df.loc[mask, 'col'] = value) reliably modifies the DataFrame in-place, unlike chained assignment.' holds in the obvious case but breaks here. Distractor B is the hard negative — the rule naively applied.
